issue: The sheet currently performs routing decisions that should belong to a shared workflow authority.
whyItMatters: PC/NPC/follower/droid/vehicle sheets and macros can drift if sheet code remains the combat router.
recommendedAlignment: Create CombatWorkflowRegistry and make sheet call registry instead of branching through every workflow itself.

issue: The mapper only returns a small subset of action fields and drops fields needed by the routing contract.
whyItMatters: Even if action data gains resolutionMode/gmManaged/executable fields, live UI cannot use them if the mapper discards them.
recommendedAlignment: Preserve source fields and add derived safe defaults.

issue: Architecture declares CombatEngine.resolveAttack as the attack SSOT, but many live paths call rollAttack/SWSERoll/FullAttackExecutor directly.
whyItMatters: Documentation and runtime behavior disagree, making fixes hard to place safely.
recommendedAlignment: Keep CombatEngine registered as an authority, but put a thin workflow registry above all live entrypoints.

issue: executeAction handles only a few special cases and is not broad enough to be the workflow router.
whyItMatters: Routing all actions to it today would produce many unimplemented or no-op cases.
recommendedAlignment: Use it as one registered fallback/special-action handler, not the registry itself.

path: multiple sheet/ui/app files calling SWSERoll.rollAttack or rollAttack directly
issue: Many entrypoints bypass action routing, action economy, and normalized context.
whyItMatters: Attack context is inconsistent by source button/sheet/app.
recommendedAlignment: Migrate callers gradually to CombatWorkflowRegistry.execute({ resolutionMode: 'attack' }).

issue: Damage formula, damage calculation, damage mutation, and manual damage UI are separate authorities without a shared packet contract.
whyItMatters: Stun/Ion/Sonic/Evasion/Burst Fire/crit context can be lost or interpreted differently.
recommendedAlignment: Do not solve in Phase 1A; preserve attack-to-damage context now and merge through damage packet model later.

issue: The roll dialog gathers useful options but returns ad hoc field names and lacks a canonical context object.
whyItMatters: Feat/talent metadata cannot wake up reliably when context names drift.
recommendedAlignment: In Phase 2, make CombatContextBuilder normalize roll-dialog output before calling resolvers.

issue: Existing action-economy authority has likely RAW seams from Phase 0H.
whyItMatters: Every action-costed combat mode depends on this math.
recommendedAlignment: Keep it as SSOT but harden before relying on it for all combat action spending.

path: sheet and subsystem direct game.settings.get calls
issue: HouseRuleService is documented as SSOT, but direct settings reads remain in runtime paths.
whyItMatters: Defaults/adapters/governance can be bypassed, especially for ammo, action economy, fight defensively, healing, and condition track settings.
recommendedAlignment: Use HouseRuleService/adapters in registry and gradually migrate direct calls.

issue: Several systems are exposed globally for macros/legacy consumers.
whyItMatters: Legacy globals can keep bypassing the workflow registry after Phase 1B.
recommendedAlignment: Keep globals for compatibility but make them call the registry or registered subsystem when touched later.
